footer.summary: Productized architecture audits for teams that need precision, speed, and a clear remediation path.

hero.description: Run a premium web performance and architecture intake in seconds. We review delivery speed, SEO resilience, frontend structure, and remediation depth.
hero.panelDescription: Use a real website URL to test validation, async states, and the production-ready fetch flow.
hero.helper: Secure intake. No source code access is required for the first pass.
hero.formCaption: Paste a production URL and preview the productized intake flow.
hero.formFootnote: The endpoint can be connected through VITE_AUDIT_ENDPOINT.
hero.successDescription: The request passed validation and the workflow is ready for a real endpoint.

validation.requiredUrl: Enter a website URL to continue.
validation.invalidUrl: Enter a valid URL that starts with http:// or https://.
validation.submitFailed: The audit request failed. Try again in a moment.

auditConsole.title: An autonomous audit console you can actually show
auditConsole.description: Turn Hermes-style orchestration into a premium mission control surface. Every subagent, tool call, and long-term memory write is visible in real time instead of being buried behind a spinner.
auditConsole.capabilitiesTitle: Expose parallel execution, subagent spawning, and persistent memory as first-class UI.

parallel.title: Parallel tool execution
parallel.description: Run DOM, API, and architecture investigations in parallel so investors and clients can watch the system work.

memory.title: Persistent memory sync
memory.description: Visualize the exact moment a runtime fact is promoted into long-term memory for later synthesis and remediation.

report.title: Streaming final report
report.description: Type the final report into view character by character so the audit feels alive instead of static.

auditConsole.helper: This console visualizes Hermes-style orchestration first and switches to real audit pipeline evidence and synthesis whenever the live result is available.

phaseDescriptions.idle: The Hermes workflow has not started yet. Submit a URL and the console will visualize how the agent coordinates the audit from planning to synthesis.
phaseDescriptions.analyzing_context: The primary agent is reviewing the target URL, runtime context, and available tools before delegating work.
phaseDescriptions.spawning_subagents: The primary agent is creating specialist subagents and binding tools to each role.
phaseDescriptions.parallel_execution: Subagents are now running in parallel. Each glass card streams its own tool output in real time.
phaseDescriptions.synthesizing_memory: The system is promoting the latest runtime finding into long-term memory so later remediation and reporting stay aligned.
phaseDescriptions.streaming_report: The primary agent is synthesizing the subagent outputs into a final report that streams directly into view.
phaseDescriptions.complete: The Hermes simulation is complete. You can open the sample report to compare the console with the evidence-driven reporting surface.

sections.spawningCardDescription: Waiting for the primary agent to finish delegation and tool binding.
sections.parallelDescription: Each glass card represents a dedicated subagent with its own live terminal output.
sections.reportDescription: This pane types out how the primary agent turns subagent evidence and memory updates into an executive summary.
sections.reportDescriptionLive: This pane streams the real audit pipeline synthesis and evidence summary instead of only showing mock output.
sections.reportDescriptionPending: The primary agent is in final reporting and is waiting for the real audit pipeline to return evidence and synthesis output.
sections.reportWaiting: Waiting for the primary agent to begin the final report stream...
sections.memoryEmpty: No long-term memory writes have been recorded yet.
sections.missionStreamDescription: Current target: {{url}}. Move through each phase to watch subagent spawning, parallel execution, and report streaming unfold.

memory.runtimeGate: Confirmed the primary runtime gate as "{{step}}" ({{status}}).
memory.runtimeGateWithDetail: Confirmed the primary runtime gate as "{{step}}" ({{status}}) at {{detail}}.
memory.responseTime: Deterministic collector recorded an initial HTML response time of {{value}} ms.
memory.pipeline: The audit pipeline is synthesizing with {{provider}} and the browser collector is running in {{mode}} mode.

frontend.responseTime: Live deterministic evidence: initial HTML returned in {{value}} ms.
frontend.responseTimeMissing: Live deterministic evidence: no initial HTML response time was captured for this run.
frontend.documentSignals: Document structure synced: {{scripts}} script tag(s), {{stylesheets}} stylesheet link(s), and {{images}} image(s).
frontend.runtimeGateWithDetail: Frontend runtime gate aligned to "{{step}}" ({{status}}) at {{detail}}.
frontend.runtimeGateMissing: Frontend evidence did not identify a primary runtime gate.

backend.statusCode: Initial response metadata: HTTP {{value}}, content type {{contentType}}.

architecture.flowMissing: No browser flow execution was available for this run.
architecture.summary: Synthesis returned a summary that can now be shared by the final report and memory panel.
architecture.summaryMissing: No synthesis summary was available, so the architecture lane is summarizing from evidence metadata only.

report.title: Hermes Live Audit Summary
report.responseTimeMissing: No deterministic response time was captured for this run.
report.runtimeGateMissing: No primary runtime gate was explicitly identified in this evidence bundle.
report.summaryMissing: No synthesis summary was available, so Hermes is only showing evidence metadata.
report.storeSynced: The latest audit result has been synced to the sample report page for direct comparison.

snapshot.title: Live evidence snapshot
snapshot.empty: No usable live audit pipeline result has arrived yet, so the console is still showing the mock report.
snapshot.gateMissing: No primary runtime gate is currently identifiable.

frontend.queue: Queueing DOM depth and hydration-boundary analysis.
frontend.dom: Homepage DOM sampling completed with no abnormal first-route expansion.
frontend.hydration: Report route still depends on prior audit hydration, so the runtime gate remains unresolved.
frontend.summary: Frontend pass completed. The main risk is still the report-route hydration gate.

backend.queue: Pinging the API gateway and establishing a latency baseline.
backend.gateway: Gateway round-trip latency remains within the acceptable range with no obvious burst pattern.
backend.waterfall: Compared the initial document waterfall with follow-up requests and did not detect a server-side choke point of the same severity.
backend.summary: API latency analysis completed. The primary issue remains the frontend runtime gate, not server saturation.

architecture.queue: Aligning cross-route state dependencies with the evidence schema.
architecture.paths: Located the report-route state boundary. Session-backed audit evidence is still the key prerequisite.
architecture.memory: Preparing a long-term memory write so remediation planning and reporting cite the same runtime fact.
architecture.summary: Architecture memory synthesis completed. The stuck runtime gate can now be explained clearly across the product.

memory.fact: The report route still depends on a session-backed audit hydration gate, and that remains the primary blocker.

report.title: Hermes Audit Summary
report.lead: Completed a three-lane parallel audit for {{url}} and converged on one primary runtime gate.
report.point1: The frontend speed subagent confirmed that the homepage and pricing route remain stable, but the report route still depends on a hydration boundary.
report.point2: The API latency subagent did not uncover an equivalent server-side bottleneck, so the next investigation should remain focused on the frontend runtime path.
report.point3: The architecture memory subagent promoted the session-backed evidence dependency into long-term memory so remediation planning and summaries can reuse it.
report.closing: Recommended next step: remove the report-route runtime hydration gate before asking browser evidence and final reporting to converge on one reliable execution path.

features.sectionDescription: A productized review focused on the bottlenecks that impact growth, delivery velocity, and platform resilience.

performance.title: Performance signals
performance.description: Core Web Vitals, rendering cost, bundle pressure, and delivery path analysis.

architecture.title: Architecture risk
architecture.description: Frontend boundaries, caching posture, API orchestration, and maintainability pressure points.

remediation.title: Actionable fixes
remediation.description: Prioritized recommendations with code-level direction your team can execute immediately.

workflow.sectionTitle: A fast path from URL to action plan
workflow.sectionDescription: The page is wired like a production intake flow, with explicit loading, error, and success states.

intake.title: Submit a live URL
intake.description: We validate the protocol format and prepare the payload for the audit service.

analysis.title: Trigger the audit pipeline
analysis.description: A simulated delay mirrors real network latency while the fetch hook stays ready for production.

delivery.title: Return clear next actions
delivery.description: The success state frames the handoff to remediation planning and reporting.

homePreview.sectionTitle: The marketing shell now extends into pricing, intake, and a sample report experience.
homePreview.sectionDescription: The design drafts are no longer isolated HTML references. They are connected as navigable React pages with shared Liquid Glass components and i18n wiring.
homePreview.secondaryTitle: Hash-based navigation keeps the prototype deployable on a static host.
homePreview.secondaryDescription: The router uses location.hash so the pages can switch without adding server-side rewrite rules during prototype review.

status.idleDescription: The form validates the URL, manages loading state, and falls back to a simulated success path when no endpoint is configured.

pricing.title: Choose the audit depth that matches your release pressure
pricing.description: Every plan includes a written executive summary, technical findings, and a remediation path sized for delivery teams.
pricing.compareDescription: Start with a lightweight signal or move straight to an architect-led remediation partnership.
pricing.deliveryTitle: A productized service, not an open-ended consulting loop
pricing.deliveryDescription: The engagement is structured to remove calendar churn. Teams submit context once, receive focused findings, and move into execution faster.
pricing.faqTitle: What teams usually ask before they buy
pricing.faqDescription: The offer is designed to be low-friction and decision-ready.

blueprint.name: Blueprint
blueprint.description: Fast signal on performance, SEO, and baseline platform risk.

optimization.name: Optimization
optimization.description: A deeper technical review for teams already feeling architecture drag.

partner.name: Partner
partner.description: For teams that need workshop support, roadmap framing, and follow-up guidance.

features.followup: Follow-up Q&A with a senior architect

delivery.question: How fast is delivery?
delivery.answer: Most audits are delivered within 48 hours after the intake is complete and access requirements are confirmed.

access.question: Do you need repository access?
access.answer: Not for the first pass. The intake starts with a live URL and platform context, then expands only if deeper review is required.

scope.question: What does the report include?
scope.answer: Each plan includes structured findings, severity framing, and concrete remediation direction sized to the selected tier.

report.title: Preview the dashboard your team receives
report.description: A concise reporting surface for executives and engineers: scores, architecture risk, and prioritized fixes.

performance.label: Performance
performance.support: Good baseline, but render blocking still hurts the first route.

seo.label: SEO posture
seo.support: Search signals are stable with room for structured data cleanup.

architecture.label: Architecture risk
architecture.support: Checkout and data orchestration need tighter boundaries.

overview.title: The audit highlights three root causes worth addressing first
overview.description: The current platform is not broadly broken. The real issue is concentrated drag in a few high-leverage paths.

items.render: The first route still carries unnecessary render-blocking scripts.
items.cache: Edge caching is inconsistent between landing and transactional routes.
items.deps: Critical views depend on cross-cutting modules with unclear ownership.

performance.title: Performance risk is tied to a narrow critical path
performance.description: The site is close to target, but the checkout and personalization surfaces concentrate the largest regressions.

items.lcp: Largest Contentful Paint regresses on template-heavy pages.
items.bundle: The application shell ships too much shared code on first paint.
items.thirdParty: Third-party tags compete for bandwidth during critical rendering.

seo.title: Search signals are healthy but still uneven
seo.description: The site has solid crawlability, but metadata discipline breaks on several dynamic templates.

items.metadata: Duplicate title and description patterns appear on filtered category views.
items.internalLinks: Important navigation paths are under-linked from commercial content.
items.structuredData: Structured data coverage is incomplete on product detail pages.

architecture.title: The architecture problem is orchestration, not raw scale
architecture.description: A few boundaries are too porous, which increases release risk and slows root-cause analysis.

items.api: Checkout flows still couple UI rendering to multiple synchronous API calls.
items.cache: Cache ownership is split across edge and application layers without a shared policy.
items.observability: Telemetry does not currently map cleanly to feature ownership.

actions.title: These fixes can move directly into a sprint backlog
actions.description: The report translates findings into execution order instead of ending at a score.

items.defer: Defer non-critical scripts until after first meaningful paint.
items.cacheHeaders: Standardize cache headers across category, product, and checkout surfaces.
items.checkout: Isolate checkout data fetching into a narrower orchestration layer.

architectureLens.title: A visual snapshot of the highest-risk dependency chain
architectureLens.description: This panel turns a complex architecture narrative into one path engineers can act on immediately.
architectureLens.issueDescription: The checkout API bypasses a stable cache layer and fans out into slow downstream reads, increasing both latency and operational noise.

actions.title: The next sprint can start here
actions.summary: The current audit maps to a practical sprint backlog, not a generic scorecard.

items.defer: Defer non-critical marketing tags on the first route.
items.cacheHeaders: Align cache headers and purge strategy across edge and app layers.
items.checkout: Refactor checkout orchestration behind a narrower API boundary.

empty.title: No live audit result yet
empty.description: Run a scan or submit the intake wizard to populate this report with real pipeline evidence.

overall.failed: Evidence capture did not complete end-to-end, so this score is provisional.
overall.withBrowser: {{warningCount}} evidence warning(s) remain after deterministic and browser collection.
overall.withPartial: Browser evidence is attached, but runtime coverage is still partial and leaves {{warningCount}} warning(s) unresolved.
overall.withoutBrowser: This score is based on deterministic evidence only; browser verification is still pending.

performance.withValue: Initial HTML responded in {{value}} ms with {{scripts}} scripts detected.
performance.missing: Performance scoring is provisional because deterministic timing data is missing.

seo.withSignals: {{missingCount}} baseline document signal(s) still need cleanup.
seo.clean: Core document signals are present in the initial HTML response.

architecture.withBrowser: Browser flows are available for runtime verification.
architecture.withPartial: Browser evidence is attached, but at least one runtime step or flow is still incomplete.
architecture.withoutBrowser: Architecture confidence is limited until browser evidence is enabled.

overview.title: Latest evidence snapshot
overview.descriptionWithSummary: This overview combines synthesis output with the latest deterministic and browser collector evidence.
overview.descriptionWithoutSummary: The evidence pipeline completed without a narrative summary, so this overview is assembled from raw collector output.
overview.summaryUnavailable: No synthesized summary was available for this run.

performance.title: Deterministic performance evidence
performance.description: This section is derived from the initial HTML response and network metadata, not a fixed placeholder set.
performance.responseTimeMissing: Initial HTML response time was not captured.
performance.scriptCount: The first document included {{value}} script tags and {{stylesheets}} stylesheet links.
performance.cacheControlMissing: No Cache-Control header was returned on the initial response.

seo.title: SEO and document signals
seo.description: Metadata, canonical tags, language hints, and structured data are taken from the deterministic document pass.
seo.metaPresent: Meta description is present in the initial document.
seo.metaMissing: Meta description is missing from the initial document.
seo.structuredDataMissing: No structured data blocks were detected.
seo.altComplete: No missing alt text was detected in the initial image set.

architecture.title: Runtime architecture evidence
architecture.descriptionWithBrowser: Architecture findings combine deterministic evidence with browser-flow observations, so the report can point at runtime boundaries instead of only HTML hints.
architecture.descriptionWithPartial: Browser evidence is available, but at least one runtime step is still incomplete or blocked, so architecture findings should be treated as provisional.
architecture.descriptionWithoutBrowser: Browser evidence is not available yet, so architecture findings currently reflect deterministic signals plus collector readiness.
architecture.browserFlowMissing: No browser flow execution was recorded for this run.

actions.title: Evidence-based next actions
actions.description: These actions are generated from the current evidence bundle, so they change with each run instead of staying fixed.
actions.actionReduceResponseTime: Reduce initial HTML response time before adding deeper browser instrumentation.
actions.actionReviewScripts: Audit first-route scripts and remove render-blocking or low-value tags.
actions.actionAddMeta: Add a stable meta description policy for key landing and transactional templates.
actions.actionAddCanonical: Define canonical ownership across dynamic or filtered routes.
actions.actionAddStructuredData: Implement structured data on high-value templates before scaling crawl exposure.
actions.actionFixAlt: Backfill missing alt text on content-critical images.
actions.actionResolveBlockedStep: Resolve the blocked browser step "{{step}}" before trusting runtime coverage for this flow.
actions.actionReviewPartialStep: Review the incomplete browser step "{{step}}" and harden its selector or state setup.
actions.actionEnableBrowser: Enable a browser collector for stateful flows before making high-confidence architecture calls.
actions.actionReviewCache: Standardize cache headers so deterministic and browser evidence converge on one policy.
actions.actionGeneral: Use this evidence bundle as the baseline for a deeper architecture review.

evidence.title: Runtime artifact readiness
evidence.description: This card exposes whether Webwright or a future browser runner actually attached pages, flows, screenshots, logs, and trace artifacts to the current report.

detail.workspaceMissing: No browser workspace directory was attached to this run.
detail.reportMissing: No report artifact path was attached.
detail.traceMissing: No trajectory or trace artifact was attached.

manifest.title: Artifact manifest
manifest.description: The first attached browser artifacts are listed here so the report shows which concrete files backed the current evidence bundle.
manifest.empty: No concrete browser artifacts were attached to this run yet.

timeline.title: Execution timeline
timeline.description: This timeline shows the parsed Webwright trajectory steps so the report reflects what the browser runner actually did.
timeline.empty: No executable trajectory steps were attached to this run.

issueTitle.general: Evidence path ready for deeper inspection

issueDescription.browserPending: The deterministic pass completed, but the browser collector is still in {{mode}} mode. This is the correct integration point for Webwright or Playwright.
issueDescription.browserPartial: Browser evidence is attached, but at least one runtime step is incomplete or blocked. Fix the failing flow before treating this run as full coverage.
issueDescription.performance: The initial HTML response and first-document payload suggest a narrow but important performance bottleneck worth instrumenting next.
issueDescription.seo: The deterministic document pass surfaced metadata or structured-data gaps that can be validated before deeper crawling.
issueDescription.general: Current evidence is enough to guide the next collector integration without returning to a fixed sample template.

intake.title: Launch your architecture audit brief
intake.description: This wizard captures the minimum context needed to turn a URL into a focused technical engagement.
intake.helper: The wizard uses real React state, step validation, and a fetch-based submit flow.
intake.summaryDescription: These answers become the architect-ready brief for the next review step.
intake.successDescription: The request cleared validation and is ready to connect to a production endpoint.
intake.followupDescription: You can now review the sample report or compare packages before connecting a real backend.

placeholders.notes: Key releases, migration plans, or pain points you want the audit to focus on.

target.title: Define goals
target.description: Capture the live target and the business pressure behind the audit.

stack.title: Map the stack
stack.description: Clarify the current frontend and delivery surfaces.

context.title: Hand off context
context.description: Provide the final detail an architect needs before the review starts.
